title: Stable Bubble Sort with Missing Swap

statement.markdown: Implement a stable bubble sort algorithm that sorts an array of integers in ascending order. The provided function contains a bug where the swap operation is missing.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a non-empty array of unique integers.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return true if the array is sorted, false otherwise.
statement.notes: The array size will be at most 10000.

examples.explanation: The input array should be sorted in ascending order.
The input array is not sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: The bubble sort algorithm is not correctly implemented.

editorial.title: Stable Bubble Sort with Missing Swap
editorial.intuition: To fix the bug, we need to correctly implement the bubble sort algorithm and swap the elements.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach for this problem would be to check each element in the array and compare it with the next one.
editorial.optimalSolution: To fix the bug, we can replace the line `int temp = a[i];` with `a[i] = a[i + 1];`. This will correctly swap the elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected algorithm will iterate through the array and swap elements until the array is sorted.
editorial.dryRun: Here is an example dry run of the algorithm: if the array is [5, 2, 8, 1, 9], the first iteration will swap the elements 5 and 2, resulting in [2, 5, 8, 1, 9].
